project_structure.description: Agentic software factory template with saga-based orchestration for Devin CLI execution. Implements graph-based workflow orchestration with step execution, verification-driven routing, and state persistence.

subprojects.description: Core saga orchestration engine with step execution, state management, and verification routing
Step definitions with prompts, agent configs, and verification scripts
Saga workflow definitions in JSON format

decisions.why: Defines the saga orchestration architecture and Devin CLI integration. Establishes graph-based execution model, traversal limits, and verification-driven routing. Critical for understanding current retry/correction patterns and session management limitations.

Defines file-based state persistence for saga execution. Specifies directory structure (.process/saga-<hash>/), state file format, and session ID capture. Directly relevant to implementing conversation accumulation pattern and file structure changes.

target_architecture.why: Documents saga enrichment patterns for context passing between steps. Relevant for understanding how accumulated conversation history will be passed to retry attempts.

components.why: Main execution engine. Contains _execute_step() method that needs modification to detect retries and call compose_retry_prompt(). Also contains _route_to_next_node() for routing logic and traversal tracking.

Step orchestrator. Contains _write_session_state() method that currently writes to .process/<step>/ or logging_dir. Needs modification to create node-specific directories and write three files (input.txt, output.txt, verification.txt). Also needs new methods: _determine_attempt_number() and compose_retry_prompt().
Devin CLI wrapper for step execution. Currently invokes agent and captures output. May need modification to support passing accumulated prompts on retry.

services.why: State management for saga execution. Tracks saga state and step execution history. May be extended to track attempt numbers and accumulated prompts.

models.why: Data structures for saga definitions including NodeDefinition, StateEntry, SubSagaEntry. May need extension for attempt tracking.

tests.why: Tests for saga execution. Will need new tests for retry logic, attempt number determination, and accumulated prompt composition.
Tests for state management. Will need tests for new state file structure and attempt tracking.

current_implementation_notes.session_management: Current implementation attempts to resume sessions via session_id.txt but Devin CLI does not provide discoverable session IDs. Session resumption is not viable.
current_implementation_notes.state_files: Currently writes stdout.txt, stderr.txt, and session_id.txt to .process/<step>/ or logging_dir. No node-specific subdirectories or attempt tracking.
current_implementation_notes.retry_pattern: Uses traversal limits on connections to prevent infinite loops. Retry logic is graph-based (routing back to same node). No conversation accumulation or context passing on retry.
current_implementation_notes.verification: Verification scripts run after step execution. Failures trigger routing decisions (pass/fail). No mechanism to pass verification feedback back to agent on retry.
current_implementation_notes.enrichment: Enrichment dictionary passed to orchestrator for prompt variable substitution. Currently supports verification_errors but no accumulated conversation history.

implementation_gaps: No node-specific directory structure for organizing attempt artifacts
No attempt number tracking or determination logic
No accumulated prompt composition from previous attempts
No mechanism to pass full conversation history to retry attempts
No clear separation between input.txt, output.txt, and verification.txt files
Session ID tracking is non-functional (Devin CLI limitation)
design_constraints: Must maintain backward compatibility with existing sagas
Must use existing traversal limit mechanism for retry enforcement
Must not require session resumption (not viable with Devin CLI)
Must preserve all attempt artifacts for debugging
Must work with current file-based state persistence approach
